title: NIS2 Supply Chain Security Assessment Coverage

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(d) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(d) (Supply-chain security) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.25: NIS2 Supply Chain Security Assessment Coverage.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.25' running on nis2_supplier_assessment_register.csv, index=github (Dependabot alerts), index=itsm, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `nis2_supplier_assessment_register.csv` lookup (supplier_id, supplier_name, tier, next_assessment_date, supplier_code_repo); `index=github` `sourcetype=dependabot_alert` (alert_number, repo, severity); `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:task` for linked remediation tasks when Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) pulls `/now/table/sn_task`.
app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)), Splunk Add-on for GitHub ([Splunkbase 5596](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5596)) with REST or webhook ingestion for Dependabot.
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_supplier_assessment_register.csv
| eval assessment_due=strptime(next_assessment_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval overdue=if(now()>assessment_due,1,0)
| join supplier_code_repo type=left [
    search index=github sourcetype="dependabot_alert" earliest=-30d
    | stats dc(alert_number) as open_supply_chain_alerts by repo
    | rename repo as supplier_code_repo
]
| fillnull value=0 open_supply_chain_alerts
| where overdue=1 OR open_supply_chain_alerts > 10
| table supplier_name, tier, next_assessment_date, overdue, open_supply_chain_alerts
description: Combines the authoritative supplier assessment register with open Dependabot-style repository alerts so procurement sees both paperwork gaps and concrete open dependency findings per supplier code base.
value: Leadership receives one numbered list that pairs "assessment overdue" with "dependency alerts still open" so board updates do not claim green third-party posture while GitHub still shows dozens of unfixed issues.
implementation:
(1) Publish `nis2_supplier_assessment_register.csv` under `lookups/` with `supplier_id` matching your CMDB key. (2) Map `supplier_code_repo` to GitHub `repo` names (`org/name`). (3) Configure GitHub TA (5596) modular input or HEC for Dependabot JSON. (4) Optional: mirror ServiceNow tasks (1928) to prove compensating documentation exists. (5) Tune threshold `open_supply_chain_alerts > 10` per tier.

**Note:** Normalize `supplier_code_repo` to lowercase `org/repo` to match GitHub `repo` before scheduling.
visualization: Table (supplier_name, tier, overdue flag, alert count), bar chart by tier, single value overdue tier-1 count.
knownFalsePositives:
• **Assessment due date mis-keyed in CSV** — Distinguish: typo pushes next_assessment_date into past. Suppress: governance process to fix lookup; interim `where supplier_name!="EXAMPLE_VENDOR"` filter.
• **Dependabot duplicates same CVE across alerts** — Distinguish: `dc(alert_number)` still inflates counts. Suppress: replace with `dc(security_advisory_id)` after field extract.
• **Supplier uses GitLab not GitHub** — Distinguish: repo field empty, join misses. Suppress: add parallel subsearch for `sourcetype=gitlab:vulnerability` or mark supplier `sca_platform=github` in register.
• **Planned acceptance of low-severity dependency noise** — Distinguish: risk acceptance memo signed for dev-only repos. Suppress: `vuln_exception_register.csv` keyed on repo with `max_cvss`.
• **Quarterly questionnaire cadence mis-aligned with CSV monthly updates** — Distinguish: real-world review happened but CSV stale <30 days. Suppress: sync ServiceNow field `u_next_assessment` nightly via scripted export.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Add-on for GitHub (Splunkbase 5596) and ServiceNow (1928) deployed to the same search head running compliance dashboards.
• Lookup `nis2_supplier_assessment_register.csv` stored under `lookups/` with permissions limited to `compliance_admin` role.
• GitHub token scopes: `repo` + `security_events` for Dependabot; rotate **every 90 days**.
• Index capacity: Dependabot alerts are bursty after org-wide rebases — allocate **5 GB/day** headroom for large dev shops.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Export supplier register fields: `supplier_id`, `supplier_name`, `tier`, `next_assessment_date`, `supplier_code_repo`. Use `inputlookup` dry run in Search to validate `strptime` on `next_assessment_date` (support optional `T` time).
Enable GitHub webhook `dependabot_alert` pointing to HEC or use TA polling every **900 seconds** hitting `api.github.com/orgs/{org}/dependabot/alerts`.
ServiceNow: enable incident/task pull focused on `supplier_security_review` topics if you need human workflow proof.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run SPL weekly (Monday 06:00) with **earliest=-30d** on subsearch to capture open alerts.
Save as `UC-22.2.25_assessment_vs_dependabot`. If join explodes, replace with `stats` + `lookup` pattern.
Add `| fillnull value=0 open_supply_chain_alerts` before final where to avoid dropping purely overdue suppliers.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick vendor `tier=1` with known overdue questionnaire in GRC; confirm `overdue=1`.
Cross-check GitHub UI Dependabot count vs `open_supply_chain_alerts` for same repo string — reconcile case sensitivity using `| eval repo=lower(repo)` upstream.
Validate ServiceNow ticket exists if compensating control claimed — search `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:task`.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: color overdue rows red, alert count amber >5. Email CSV to procurement DL via scheduled PDF.
Escalation matrix: tier-1 suppliers page CISO secondary on-call.
Archive `lookup` versions weekly into git with hash referenced in audit workbook.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **join yields zero alerts** — field mismatch; ensure `supplier_code_repo` matches GitHub `repo`.
• **Subsearch hits limit** — raise subsearch `maxout` in limits.conf (change control) or narrow earliest window.
• **Token 401** — GitHub PAT expired; TA logs in `splunkd.log` under RestHandler.
• **CSV strptime fail** — dates use `DD/MM/YYYY`; update `strptime` format.
• **mass FP after tooling upgrade** — Dependabot introduced new severity field — update stats to filter `state=open` only.
grandmaExplanation: We line up the paperwork deadlines for checking outside suppliers with the real open problems reported in the code libraries those suppliers help maintain, so nobody thinks the homework is done when the toolbox is still messy.
exclusions: Does not determine legal applicability, complete regulator submissions, approve policy decisions, or replace counsel/competent-authority review.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.2.25` output, Dependabot reconciliation worksheet, and signed lookup version hash stored with the NIS2 supplier risk evidence bundle.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Populate the lookup with a tier-1 supplier whose `next_assessment_date` is seven days in the past and ensure the GitHub subsearch returns twelve open `dependabot_alert` events; the combined row appears with `overdue=1` and `open_supply_chain_alerts` above the threshold.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Use the same supplier row but refresh `next_assessment_date` to ninety days ahead and reduce Dependabot fixtures below three open alerts; the scheduled search produces zero rows and the audit log records a clean run.
